question: What does the book of Acts begin with a reference to (Acts 1:1)?
options: The author’s location in Asia Minor
The author’s role as a historian of the church

question: To whom is the book of Acts written (Acts 1:1)?
What was the writer of Acts’ former book about (Acts 1:1)?
options: All Jesus began to do and teach
The travels of Jesus in Galilee and Judea
The life and death of Jesus

question: What did Jesus give to the apostles after he rose from the dead (Acts 1:3)?
options: Many convincing proofs that he was alive
Many signs and wonders so they would believe in him
The gift of the Holy Spirit
A commission to spread the good news

question: For how many days did the resurrected Jesus appear to his apostles (Acts 1:3)?
After the resurrection, to what did Jesus compare John’s baptism with water (Act 1:5)?
options: The coming baptism with the Holy Spirit
The coming baptism of persecution they would face
The coming baptism by fire of the church
The coming of fire on them to teach God’s word to the nations

question: What did Jesus say was not for the disciples to know (Acts 1:7)?
options: Why Jesus had to die in order for sins to be forgiven
When the Holy Spirit would come upon them in Jerusalem
The dates or times the Father has set by his authority
When Jesus would return to rule over the earth

question: What would the apostles do when the Holy Spirit comes on them (Acts 1:8)?
options: They would become Jesus’ witnesses to the ends of the earth
They would speak the word of God with boldness
They would build the church of God with both Jews and Gentiles
They would forgive the sins of many

question: What blocked their view when Jesus was taken up (ascension) (Acts 1:9)?
How far is the Mount of Olives from Jerusalem (Acts 1:12)?
All the following were listed as apostles EXCEPT (Acts 1:13)
How did Peter describe Judas after the ascension (Acts 1:16)?
options: One who served as a guide for those who arrested Jesus
One who served among us but was not one of us
The one who betrayed Jesus for thirty pieces of silver

question: What did Akeldama the field purchased by Judas mean (Acts 1:19)?
The “Field of Blood” purchased by Judas was call in Aramaic ________ (Acts 1:19)
What must the new apostle chosen be able to have been a witness of (Acts 1:22)?
Who was the one apostle proposed who did not get the position (Acts 1:23)?
When were the apostles all together in one place (Acts 2:1)?
On Pentecost, what sound did they hear (Acts 2:2)?
options: A sound like a violent wind
A sound like the crashing sea

question: On Pentecost, what did they see (Acts 2:3)?
options: Tongues of fire resting on each of them
The Spirit of God descend like a dove
The glory of God in the heavens

question: At Pentecost, who was staying at Jerusalem and were utterly amazed (Acts 2:5)?
options: Travelers from all the provinces of Rome
Pilgrims coming to Jerusalem for the festival

question: What did each language group hear the apostles speak about (Acts 2:11)?
options: The gospel of the kingdom of God

question: What prophet predicted of the events that happened at Pentecost (Acts 2:16)?
Upon whom did Joel say God would pour out his Spirit (Acts 2:17)?
What would be the result of the Spirit being poured out on your servants (Acts 2:18)?
options: They would speak with other tongues
They would all believe in the Lord

question: Who will be saved on the day of the Lord according to Joel (Acts 2:21)?
options: Those who follow the Lamb of God
Who repent and believe on the gift of God
Those who call on the name of the Lord

question: What did Peter say could not keep a hold on Jesus (Acts 2:24)?
Why did David say he would not be shaken (Acts 2:25)?
options: Because he believed in the Son of God
Because he will not fear what men can do unto him
Because the Lord is at David’s right hand
Because the Spirit has given him life

question: Why did David say he would live in hope (Acts 2:27)?
options: Because all men would seek him
Because the Spirit would never leave him
Because he would someday see God
Because God would not abandon him to the grave

question: What did Peter say “we are all witnesses of” (Acts 2:32)?
Peter announced that Jesus was exalted ________ (Acts 2:33)
options: To the New Jerusalem in heaven
To the right hand of God

question: Who did David say talked to his Lord (Acts 2:35)?
options: The ruler of the kingdom of heaven

question: What did Peter say all Israel should be assured of (Acts 2:36)?
options: Christ was coming back from the Father
God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ
God had given Christ the gift of the Holy Spirit
Christ will rule with a rod of iron

question: Peter told the Jews what baptism should they be baptized with (Acts 2:38)?
What did Peter warn the people at Pentecost to save themselves from (Acts 2:40)?
options: The responsibility of killing the Lord of glory

question: How many were baptized on Pentecost after Peter’s sermon (Acts 2:41)?
What was characteristic of the first believers after Pentecost (Acts 2:45)?
options: They left Jerusalem to spread the good news to the nations
They sold all their goods and gave to anyone as he had need
They were taught by the apostles to believe in Jesus
They prayed and fasted for the seven days of the feast

question: Who went to the temple at the time of prayer (Acts 3:1)?
Who did Peter and John meet by the temple gate called Beautiful (Acts 3:2)?
Why was the crippled man placed by the temple gate (Acts 3:2)?
options: To hear the teaching of the Scriptures
To be cleansed by the priests
To beg from those going into the temple

question: What did Peter tell the crippled man at the temple he did not have (Acts 3:6)?
Why did Peter tell them they needed to repent and turn to God (Acts 3:19)?
options: So that the times of refreshing may come
So that the Father might send Jesus back
So that the Spirit might live in their lives
So that the Gentiles might inherit eternal life

question: What promise given to Abraham did Peter conclude his sermon with (Acts 3:25)?
options: His seed would crush the head of the serpent
Those obeying the law would be blessed
Through his offspring all the peoples would be blessed
Through Sarah would come one who would rule over Israel forever

question: What did the captain of the temple guard and others do to Peter and John (Acts 4:3)?
options: They threw them out of the temple area
They seized them and put them in prison
They warned them if they talked in Jesus’ name they would be punished
They were dragged away and thrown outside the city gate

question: What is given that we may be saved according to Peter (Acts 4:12)?
options: Repentance for the forgiveness of sins

question: Who said through the Holy Spirit, “why do the nations rage” (Acts 4:25)?
Against whom did David say the kings of the earth take their stand (Acts 4:26)?
options: Against the Son of Man and the One sending him
Against the God of heaven and earth
Against the Lord and against his Anointed One

question: How does Acts describe the unity of the early church (Acts 4:32)?
options: They were of one heart and mind
There was no division among them

question: How did the early church view their possessions (Acts 4:32)?
options: They requested help for the widows and orphans among them
No one claimed any of his possessions as his own
They gave all their possessions to the poor
All of them gave their possessions to the apostles

question: Of what did the apostles testify in the early church (Acts 4:33)?
options: To the ascension of Jesus to the right hand of the Father
Concerning the ways Jesus fulfilled the Scriptures
To the resurrection of the Lord Jesus
To the power of the Holy Spirit

question: What were not found among the members of the early church (Acts 4:34)?
options: Any who did not believe in the resurrection of Jesus
Any who were not loved by the community

question: What does Barnabas mean (Acts 4:36)?
Where was Barnabas from (Acts 4:36)?
Barnabas was from what tribe of Israel (Acts 4:36)?
What did Barnabas do initially (Acts 4:37)?
options: Sold a field and laid the money at the apostles’ feet
Sold his house and gave the money to feed the poor among them
Left his position as a ruler of the Jews and served the apostles
Came and sat at the feet of the apostles

question: Who was Ananias’ wife (Acts 5:1)?
What did Ananias and Sapphira do (Acts 5:1)?
options: They sold a piece of property
They went with the apostles to the temple

question: What did Ananias do with the money from the property he sold (Acts 5:2)?
options: Kept some for himself and brought the rest to the apostles
Kept the whole amount and was unwilling to share any of it
Donated it to the poor at the pool of Bethesda
Gave half of it to his wife and the other half to the apostles

question: Peter said what was true after Ananias sold his property (Acts 5:4)?
options: The money was at his disposal
The money should have been given to the poor
The money would buy redemption for his soul if he had given all of it

question: After Ananias died, what did Peter ask Sapphira (Acts 5:7)?
options: Why have you withheld some of the money?
Is this how you show your commitment to Jesus?
Is this the price you got for the land?
Have you received the gift of the Holy Spirit?

question: How did the apostles who were arrested get out of jail (Acts 5:19)?
options: There was an earthquake which opened the door
An angel of the Lord opened the doors of the jail
Some of the disciples came at night and released them
They escaped out the window of the jail

question: Who was puzzled on hearing the report that no one was found in the jail (Acts 5:24)?
options: The elders of the people and the teachers of the law
The chief priests and captain of the temple guard

question: What did the high priest accuse the apostles of teaching in Jesus’ name (Acts 5:28)?
options: That they were guilty of Jesus’ blood
That they had illegally crucified the Lord of glory
That they had had them arrested illegally
That the Romans were not responsible for Jesus’ death

question: What advice did Gamaliel give to the Sanhedrin (Acts 5:38)?
options: Beat these men and warn them that they should not speak in that name
Leave these men alone, if it is of human origin it will fail
Put them in prison for three days then release them
Throw them out of the synagogues and out of Jerusalem

question: What happened to the apostles after Gamaliel’s speech to the Sanhedrin (Acts 5:40)?
options: They were released unharmed and rejoined the other disciples
They were thrown out of the city of Jerusalem and told not to come back
They were flogged and commanded not to speak in the name of Jesus
They were beaten and put back into prison for seven days

question: Why did the Grecian Jews complain against the Hebraic Jews (Acts 6:1)?
options: Because they could were not allowed into the temple courts
Because they were neglected in leadership roles in the church
Because their widows were overlooked in the daily distributions of food
Because their children were not allowed to attend the synagogues

question: What were the two requirements cited for the choosing of the seven (Acts 6:3)?
options: Respected and witnesses of the resurrection
Full of the Spirit and wisdom

question: What did the apostles want to give themselves to after choosing the seven (Acts 6:4)?
options: Breaking bread and baptizing new converts
Prayer and the ministry of the word

question: Nicholas, one of the chosen seven, was described as ______ (Acts 6:6)
options: From Antioch and a convert to Judaism
A man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit
A leader among the new converts in Jerusalem

question: How was Stephen described when he was first chosen as one of the seven (Acts 6:5)?
options: From Antioch and a convert to Judaism
A man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit
A leader among the new converts in Jerusalem

question: What did the apostles do when the seven chosen were presented to them (Acts 6:6)?
options: They invited them to sit under the teaching of Peter
They prayed for them and laid hands on them
They interviewed them and called for the Holy Spirit to dwell on them
They prayed and fasted five days

question: Stephen is described in all of the following ways EXCEPT (Acts 6:8)?
options: A man full of God’s grace
Did great wonders and miraculous signs

question: What did some of the men say against Stephen (Acts 6:11)?
options: He spoke words of blasphemy against Moses and God
He taught that Jesus was one of God’s sons
He worked on the Sabbath and fed others on that day
He violated the temple by bringing a Gentile into it

question: Who brought Stephen in front of the Sanhedrin (Acts 6:12)?
options: The elders and teachers of the law
The chief priests and captain of the temple guard

question: What did the false witnesses in the Sanhedrin accuse Stephen of (Acts 6:13)?
options: Healing a man on the Sabbath and not paying the temple tax
Claiming Jesus was coming back to judge them
Saying Jesus would destroy this place
Defiling the Sabbath and claiming that Jesus was sent from God

question: What did the false witnesses against Stephen claim Stephen taught (Acts 6:14)?
options: That they were guilty of killing Jesus of Nazareth
That Jesus had risen from the dead
That this place would be judged for condemning the Lord of glory
To change the customs Moses handed down to them

question: How was Stephen described as the Sanhedrin members looked at him (Acts 6:15)
options: With the face like the face of an angel
With a resolute look on his face
With eyes of fire from the Holy Spirit

question: Who asked Stephen if the charges against him were true (Acts 7:1)?
options: The captain of the temple guard
The chief elder of the Sanhedrin

question: In Stephen’s speech to whom did he say God appeared to in Mesopotamia (Acts 7:2)?
In Stephen’s speech, what land did Abraham leave (Acts 7:3)?
In Stephen’s speech, what covenant did he say God gave to Abraham (Acts 7:8)?

In Stephen’s speech, who was the direct son of Isaac (Acts 7:8)?
In Stephen’s speech, who was the father of the twelve patriarchs (Acts 7:8)?
In Stephen’s speech, who was sold into Egypt (Acts 7:9)?
In Stephen’s speech, who was God with (Acts 7:9)?
In Stephen’s speech, to whom did God give wisdom (Acts 7:10)?

options: When they could not find food because of the famine
When they wanted to trade the Egyptians for the crops of Canaan
When they were driven out by the Canaanites

question: In Stephen’s speech, who did Abraham buy the tomb for his sons from (Acts 7:16)?
In Stephen’s speech, who brought Moses up as a child (Acts 7:21)?

options: He left the house of Pharaoh’s daughter in order to suffer with the Israelites
He killed an Egyptian who was mistreating an Israelite
He went into the desert in search of God
God spoke to him in a burning bush that did not burn

question: In Stephen’s speech, what did the Israelites ask Aaron to do for them (Acts 7:40)?
options: Rule over them in place of Moses
Make gods who would go before them
Give them water in the desert

question: In Stephen’s speech, what did David ask for (Acts 7:46)?
options: That he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob
That he might be forgiven for his sin with Bathsheba
That he might be escape from the hands of Saul the king of Israel

question: When Stephen looked up to heaven just before he died, what did he see (Acts 7:55)?
options: Jesus in a chariot of fire
Jesus standing at the right hand of God
The Holy Spirit descending like a dove
The glory of God leaving the temple area

question: What was the last thing Stephen said before he died (Acts 7:60)?
options: Lord, do not hold this sin against them
The Lord judge between you and me

question: Who specifically was noted as giving approval to Stephen’s death (Acts 8:1)?
What happened on the day of Stephen’s martyrdom (Acts 8:1)?
options: Great persecution broke out against the church at Jerusalem
The disciples were in one place praying and praising God
The chief priests went to Pilate seeking an edict to kill Christians
The believers met together on the Mount of Olives

question: After the death of Stephen, Saul did all of the following EXCEPT (Acts 8:3)?
options: Went house to house searching for Christians
Dragged off men and women putting them in prison
Had the leaders of the church flogged

question: Who had practiced sorcery for some time in the city of Samaria (Acts 8:9)?
At what was Simon the sorcerer astonished (Acts 8:13)?
options: That such power had been given to men
By the great signs and miracles Philip did
By the witness of the resurrection of Jesus

question: What did Peter and John do when they arrived in Samaria (Acts 8:15)?

options: Tongues of fire came to rest on their shoulders after they believed

question: At the end, why did Simon the sorcerer ask Peter to pray for him (Acts 8:24)?
options: So that nothing Peter had said would happen to him
So that he might turn from his sins to believe in Jesus
So that Peter might understand how much he valued the Holy Spirit
So that God might hear his plea for forgiveness

options: He was learned in the Scriptures
He was a eunuch over the treasury

question: When did the Ethiopian ask Philip if he could be baptized (Acts 8:36)?
options: When they came to the pool of Bethesda
When they came to the Nile River
When they came to some water

The palace of Hazael overlooking Damascus

question: What city was Saul from originally (Acts 9:11)?
What had Ananias heard about Saul (Acts 9:13)?

The harm he had done to God’s saints in Jerusalem

H was going all over the world persecuting Christians

question: What had Ananias heard about Saul (Acts 9:14)?
options: He was a Pharisee who had studied at the feet of Gamaliel
How he had had Stephen stoned to death in Jerusalem
How he was going all over the world persecuting Christians

options: He received instruction about Jesus and his resurrection
He took the Lord support with the believers there
He went out into the desert to be alone with the Lord

question: How did Saul escape being killed at Damascus (Acts 9:25)?
options: He escaped on a raft in the river going under the wall of the city
His followers lowered him in a basket through an opening in the wall
His followers disguised him and hid him in a crowd leaving the city
His followers gave him a horse with which he could leave the city at night

question: Who introduced Saul to the apostles in Jerusalem (Acts 9:27)?

options: In the name of Jesus come out of him
He put mud on his eyes and told him to wash
He blew into his ears so that he could hear the good news
Jesus had healed him take care of his mat

question: Who always helped the poor continually doing good in Joppa (Acts 9:36)?

options: She did not have enough to support her family
She persecuted the church in Joppa

question: What town did Dorcas live in (Acts 9:36)?

options: Peter’s preaching the kingdom of God
Peter’s healing of the lame man

question: How did Cornelius describe to Peter the angel who had appeared to him (Acts 10:30)?
options: A man with a voice like thunder

question: What happened as Peter was speaking to Cornelius and his guests (Acts 10:44)?
options: The blind and lame were healed
The Holy Spirit came on all who heard the message

question: Why did those in Judea criticize Peter after the situation with Cornelius (Acts 11:2)?
options: That he spoke to a Roman centurion
That he accepted a Roman as a believer
That he entered the house and ate with an uncircumcised man
That he advocated paying taxes to Rome

question: What did Peter say had never entered his mouth (Acts 11:8)?
options: Anything that was not properly cooked
Anything prohibited by the law of God

question: What did the voice from heaven tell Peter the second time (Acts 11:9)?
options: Again I say, kill and eat for who made you a judge of clean and unclean
Do not refuse the voice of the Lord
Do not call impure what God has made clean

question: What did the angel tell Cornelius according to Peter (Acts 11:13)?
options: Invite Peter and his brothers into your house
Send to Joppa for Simon who is called Peter
Prepare a meal for Peter and his brothers
Ask Peter what you must do to be saved

question: How did Peter describe the coming of the Holy Spirit on Cornelius (Acts 11:15)?
options: The Spirit came on them in power and might
The Spirit manifested himself through them with signs and wonders
The Spirit came on them as he had come on us at the beginning
Their spirit spoke with the Spirit in us that they were the children of God

question: After going to Antioch why did Barnabas go to Tarsus (Acts 11:25)?
options: To spread the good news of the kingdom there
To see the Gentile believers there
To prepare the way for Peter and John to go there

question: What did Barnabas do once he had found Saul at Tarsus (Acts 11:26)?
options: He taught him the way of the Lord

question: What was credited to the church at Antioch that was a “first” (Acts 11:26)?
options: Jews and Gentiles were first considered brothers there
Disciples were first called Christians there
The church set up there without persecution
The apostles first became head of the church there

question: Who did Herod Agrippa I put to death by the sword (Acts 12:2)?

options: Had him flogged in front of all the people
Took him before the Sanhedrin for judgment
Gave him over to the high priest

question: What happened to the soldiers who had been guarding Peter (Acts 12:19)?
options: They were sent to Galilee by Herod Agrippa
They were executed by Herod Agrippa
They were shamed and sent back to Rome
They were demoded and imprisoned themselves

question: Where had Herod Agrippa gone quarreling with the people of Tyre (Acts 12:19)?

options: The Gentiles of Galilee and Dalmanutha
The people of Joppa and Caesarea

question: What happened to Herod because he did not give glory to God (Acts 12:23)?
options: He was struck dumb and unable to speak
He became unable to move like a rock and later died
He became insane and was driven out from before the people
An angel struck him and he was eaten by worms

question: What types of people were specifically listed as being in Antioch (Acts 13:1)?

What was Bar-Jesus also called (Acts 13:6)?

options: A Jewish sorcerer and false prophet

question: Who was the proconsul at Paphos (Acts 13:7)?

You are an enemy of everything that is right

options: He sat down and began to speak
He stood next to and under the synagogue leader
He sat with the people around him

question: In Paul’s sermon at Psidian Antioch, who was made king after Saul (Acts 13:22)?

question: In Paul’s sermon at Psidian Antioch, he notes whose body decayed (Acts 13:36)?

All those who were sick and in need

question: Who did Paul meet at Lystra (Acts 14:8)?

options: Because he was the chief speaker
Because he was the one who healed the lame man
Because he was shorter than Barnabas

question: What did the priest of Zeus want to do for Paul and Barnabas (Acts 14:13)?

Give them gifts of gold, frankincense and myrrh

question: Who came to Lystra resulting in Paul being stoned (Acts 14:19)?

question: What happened to Paul at Lystra on the first missionary journey (Acts 14:19)?

He was flogged with an iron tipped whip
He and Barnabas were thrown out of the city

question: What did some who came down from Jerusalem teach at Antioch in Syria (Acts 15:1)?
options: One must be baptized to be saved
One must be circumcised to be saved
One must make a pilgrimage to Jerusalem to be saved
One must obey the words of Jesus to be saved

question: How did Peter say God showed that he accepted the Gentiles as believers (Acts 15:8)?
options: Because they believed that Jesus was the Son of God
Because God gave them the Holy Spirit
Because they did signs and wonders

question: How did Peter say God purified the hearts of the Gentiles (Acts 15:9)?

question: What was Judas’ other name who was sent from Jerusalem to Antioch (Acts 15:22)?

question: Who did Paul meet at Lystra on his second missionary journey (Acts 16:1)?
What town was Timothy from (Acts 16:1)?
What did everyone in the area know about Timothy (Acts 16:3)?
options: That he was a man full of the Holy Spirit
That he often prayed and fasted

question: In Paul’s vision at Troas, what did the man in the vision beg of Paul (Acts 16:9)?
options: Come to Ephesus and teach us the good news
Come to Philippi and heal our sick
Come to Corinth and help with the conflict in our church

question: Where did Paul sail to from Samothrace second missionary journey (Acts 16:11)?

question: What profession did Lydia of Philippi have (Acts 16:14)?

options: The governor of the district of Macedonia
Some children of the servant of the governor

question: What was noted about the slave girl that followed Paul and company (Acts 16:16)?
options: She earned a great deal of money for her owners
She would cut herself morning and evening

question: What did the fortune telling slave women shout as she followed Paul (Acts 16:17)?
options: These men are against our god Artemis
These men are turning the world upside down
These men have come to enslave you
These men are servants of the Most High God

question: What did the magistrates at Philippi order be done to Paul and Silas (Acts 16:22)?
options: Put in prison and given no food
Be brought back for examination at a later time

question: What miracle happened when Paul and Silas were in the Philippian jail (Acts 16:26)?
options: There was a flame of fire
The guards became as dead men

question: What did the Philippian jailer ask Paul and Silas after the earthquake (Acts 16:30)?
options: What must I do to be saved?
What God can do such things?
What is this good news of which you speak?

question: What caused the magistrates at Philippi to be alarmed (Acts 16:38)?
options: When they saw the damage the earthquake had done to the jail
When they heard Paul and Silas were Roman citizens
When they saw the people following Paul and Silas
When they realized Paul and Silas were innocent

question: What was Paul’s “custom” when he went into a new town (Acts 17:2)?
options: He went to the home of believers there
He went to the city gate
He brought gifts to the town magistrates

question: How did the Jews at Thessalonica oppose Paul and Silas (Acts 17:5)?
options: They accused Paul of blasphemy against the gods
They brought charges against them in front of the city magistrate
They formed a mob and started a riot
They had Paul and Silas publically beaten

question: What was said of the Bereans (Acts 17:11)?
options: They rejected the word of God more than the Thessalonians had
They were of more noble character than the Thessalonians
They were more diverse than the Thessalonians
There were more Romans in Berea than in Thessalonica

question: Why did the Bereans examine Scripture every day (Acts 17:11)?
options: To see what was required of them
To understand what the prophets had said about Jesus
To see if what Paul said was true
To learn the ways of the Lord

question: What did the Jews from Thessalonica do when they came to Berea (Acts 17:13)?
options: They dragged Paul before the magistrate there
They argued with Paul in the synagogue
They accused Paul of opposing Caesar
They agitated the crowds stirring them up

question: Who stayed behind at Berea (Acts 17:14)?
Where did Paul go by himself after Berea (Act 17:15)?
What philosophical groups interacted with Paul in Athens (Acts 17:18)?
How does the text of Acts characterize the Athenians (Acts 17:21)?
options: They spend all their time discussing philosophy and analyzing arguments
They spend all their time talking and listening to the latest ideas
They spend all their time worshipping Greek gods
They spend all their time debating politics

question: What did Paul tell the Athenians about where God is (Acts 17:24)?
options: Not in temples built by human hands
Not on the tops of mountains
Not hovering over altars where sacrifices are made
He is everywhere at the same time

question: What did Paul tell the Athenians that God gives to people (Acts 17:25)?
What did Paul tell the Athenians God had determined for humans (Acts 17:26)?
options: Their set times and places to live

question: Who did Paul meet at Corinth (Acts 18:2)?
Where was Aquila originally from (Acts 18:2)?
Who was Aquila’s wife (Acts 18:2)?
What had Emperor Claudius ordered that impacted Priscilla and Aquila (Acts 18:2)?
options: All Christians were to be killed
The property of all Jews could be seized
All Jews were to leave Rome
All citizens had to serve in the Roman army

question: What trade did Paul share and work at with Aquila at Corinth (Act 18:3)?
options: He was a weaver of cloth

question: Who rejoined Paul when he was at Corinth (Act 18:5)?
Where did Silas and Timothy come from as they rejoined Paul at Corinth (Acts 18:5)?
What did Paul do after Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia (Acts 18:5)?
options: Paul rejoiced and invited them to stay with Aquila
Paul trained them in tent-making with Aquila
Paul sent them back to Athens to encourage the believers there
Paul devoted himself exclusively to preaching

question: Who was Crispus at Corinth (Acts 18:8)?
options: A ruler of the Sanhedrin there

question: How long did Paul stay at Corinth (Acts 18:11)?
Who was the proconsul of Achaia seated at Corinth (Acts 18:12)?
Gallio was proconsul over what provincial area of Greece (Acts 18:12)?
What had Paul done at Cenchrea (Acts 18:18)?
options: Cut off his hair as a result of taking a vow
Met with his brothers Silas and Timothy

question: Who was a learned Jewish man who came to Ephesus (Acts 18:24)?
Having come to Ephesus, where was Apollos from (Acts 18:24)?
What did Paul ask the Ephesian disciples when he arrived there (Acts 19:2)?
options: Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed
Did you believe on the Lord Jesus Christ
Did you realize that Jesus died and rose again
Did you hear about the kingdom of God within you

question: What did the Ephesian disciples tell Paul they had not heard of (Acts 19:2)?
Who did John the Baptist tell people to believe in (Acts 19:4)?
options: The one who is greater than he
The one who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days
The one baptizing with the Holy Spirit
The one who was coming after him

question: How were the Ephesian believers baptized a second time (Acts 19:5)?
options: Into the water and the blood of Jesus
In the name of the Lord Jesus

question: When did the Holy Spirit come on the Ephesian disciples (Acts 19:6)?
options: When Paul laid his hands on them
When they confessed Jesus as Lord

question: When the Holy Spirit came on the Ephesian disciples, what did they do (Acts 19:6)?
options: They were baptized in the name of Jesus
They were able to heal the sick among them
They spoke in tongues and prophesied

question: How long did Paul speak in the synagogue at Ephesus (Acts 19:8)?
How did some Jews try to duplicate Paul’s miracles (Acts 19:13)?
options: Commanding the sickness to leave a person in the name of Jesus
Laying hands on sick people attempting to heal them
Trying to drive out evil spirits in the name of the Lord Jesus
Trying to heal the sick by anointing them with oil

question: How did some Jews try to cast demons out of people (Acts 19:13)?
options: In the name of the Lord Jesus whom Paul preaches
By commanding them to come out by the Holy Spirit
By anointing them with oil in Jesus name
By chaining them up in prison until the demon comes out

question: What did those who had practiced sorcery do at Ephesus (Acts 19:19)?
options: They attacked Paul seeking to drive him out of the city
They sought to pay money to acquire the powers Paul had

question: What was the value of the sorcery scrolls that were burned at Ephesus (Acts 19:19)?
While at Ephesus, how did Paul decide to go to Jerusalem (Acts 19:21)?
Who was the silversmith at Ephesus who caused a riot against the Way (Acts 19:24)?
Where did the rioters at Ephesus rush as one man (Acts 19:29)?
options: Into the palace of the magistrate

question: How did the city clerk describe the image of Artemis (Act 19:35)?
options: As lord of heaven and earth

question: After the riot in Ephesus, where did Paul set out for next (Acts 20:1)?
Who was seated in the window at Troas as Paul talked on and on (Act 20:9)?
What happened to Eutychus at Troas on Paul’s third missionary journey (Acts 20:9)?
options: He fell asleep and then fell out the window as Paul spoke
He was thrown into prison as a result of Jewish persecution
He returned to Thessalonica to raise money for the poor in Jeusalem
He went and told the Jews where Paul was staying

question: What did Paul do when he found Eutychus had fallen to his death (Acts 20:10)?
options: He anointed him with oil and he came back alive
He threw himself on the young man and he came back alive
He laid his hands on his head and he came back alive
He prayed over him and he came back alive

question: Why did Paul sail past Ephesus on the third missionary journey (Acts 20:16)?
options: Because he feared another riot there
Because he was in a hurry to get to Jerusalem
Because he did not want to stir up trouble there
Because the elders at Antioch had written him to come back there

question: Who did Paul meet with at Miletus on the third missionary journey (Acts 20:17)?
options: The leaders of the synagogue there

question: What did Paul say the Holy Spirit warned him about in every city (Acts 20:23)?
options: Prison and hardships were facing him
Ultimately he would die by the sword
Rejection and opposition will be his lot

question: What news did Paul announce to the Ephesian elders at Miletus (Acts 20:25)?
options: He was taking money to the poor in Jerusalem
He would ultimately end up in Caesar’s court
None of them would see him again
They would receive his letter once he arrived at Jerusalem

question: Paul tells the Ephesian elders at Miletus that they should be _______ (Acts 20:28)?
options: Shepherds of the church of God
Doorkeepers in the house of God
Guardians of the kingdom of God

question: With whom did Paul and Luke stay at Caesarea (Acts 21:8)?
Who was Agabus that met Paul and Luke at Caesarea (Acts 21:11)?
options: An elder from the town of Ptolemais

question: What did Agabus do in front of Paul (Acts 21:12)?
options: Took a yoke and put it on his neck
Took Paul’s belt and bound his own hands and feet
Took a set of chains and chained himself to a wall
Took a key and locked them inside a room unable to escape

question: What did Agabus’ binding of himself mean (Acts 21:11)?
options: That Paul would be bound and handed over to the Gentiles
That Paul would be put in prison and unable to escape for 3 months
That Paul would be bound and dragged through the streets
That Paul would be bound and thrown out of the city

question: Who accompanied Paul and Luke when they went up to Jerusalem (Acts 21:16)?
options: Some of the messengers from Jerusalem
A centurion who was a believer
Some of the disciples from Caesarea

question: Whose house did Paul and Luke stay at in Jerusalem (Acts 21:16)?
Tarsus is in what province (Act 21:39)?
In the speech to the rioters in Jerusalem, where did Paul say he was born (Acts 22:2)?
How did Paul get out of being flogged after the riot in Jerusalem (Acts 22:25)?
options: He told the commander that the Jews were lying about him
He told the high priest that he followed the law of Moses
He told the guard that he was falsely accused
He told the centurion that he was a Roman citizen

question: How did Paul tell the commander he had become a Roman citizen (Acts 22:28)?
options: He was received it after having fought for Rome
He paid a lot of money for it
He had to swear allegiance before the Senate at Rome

question: What group holds there is no resurrection from the dead (Acts 23:8)?
The Sadducees hold that there are no _________ (Acts 23:8)
options: Books beyond the law of Moses as God’s word

question: When did Claudius Lysias sent Paul to Governor Felix at Caesarea (Acts 23:30)?
options: When he saw that they could not protect Paul from the crowds
When he heard there was a plot against Paul
When he realized it was a matter of the Jewish laws
When he saw the rioters get out of control

question: How did Paul travel the trip from Antipatris to Caesarea (Acts 23:33)?
options: On a cart with the soldiers
He was carried by the soldiers

question: When did Felix agree to hear Paul’s case in Caesarea (Acts 23:35)?
options: When Herod Agrippa came from Galilee
When he received the charges from Claudius Lysias of Jerusalem

question: What does the narrator of Acts say concerning Felix’s knowledge (Acts 24:22)?
options: Felix did not want to get into an argument over Jewish teachings
Felix was not aware of the teachings of the Way
Felix was well acquainted with the Way
Felix’s wife was a member of the Way

question: Felix adjourned the meeting with Paul and the Jews until ________ (Acts 24:22)?
options: Proper charges could be made against Paul
He received a bribe from Paul
He could write a letter with charges to be sent to Caesar

question: What was Governor Felix hoping from Paul’s trial (Acts 24:26)?
options: He was hoping he would be transferred to a different district
He was hoping that the Jews would kill Paul
He was hoping to receive a bribe from Paul
He was hoping the Jews would not bring charges against Paul

question: Why did Governor Felix leave Paul in prison for two years (Acts 24:27)?
options: He wanted to grant a favor to the Jews
He wanted to secure the proper charges against Paul
He wanted to procrastinate his decision
He wanted to listen to Paul more and more

question: Who visited Governor Festus in Caesarea (Acts 25:13)?
options: The high priest Ananias and Lysias the Roman commander
A Roman legate and his wife

question: How did Agrippa respond to Festus telling him about Paul’s situation (Acts 25:22)?
options: He said Caesar would know what to do with Paul
He said he had heard that Paul was a troublemaker
He said he would like to hear Paul himself
He said many of the followers of the Way had been condemned

question: Why did Festus say he had Paul incarcerated in Caesarea (Acts 25:24)?
options: The whole Jewish community petitioned him say Paul ought not live
The chief priests accused him of defiling the temple
The Jewish leaders condemned him as teaching against their law
Some accused him of eating with Gentiles and sinners

question: Why did Governor Festus want Agrippa’s input in Paul’s case (Acts 25:26)?
options: Festus wanted a second opinion on whether he should kill Paul
Festus wanted more protection of Paul because of threats from the Jews
Festus had nothing definite to write to Caesar about Paul
Festus wanted Agrippa to render judgment on Paul instead of Caesar

question: What did Festus thing unreasonable (Acts 25:27)?
options: To send only religious charges against Paul
To bother Caesar with such trivial matters
To ask for Caesar’s help when Paul had done nothing wrong
To send a prisoner with no charges

question: Why did Paul tell Herod Agrippa he felt fortunate (Acts 26:2)?
options: Because he was a Roman citizen
Because he was standing before Agrippa
Because he had already got to speak to Felix and Festus
Because he hoped he would soon be speaking with Caesar

question: What did Paul tell Agrippa Jesus said to Paul on the road to Damascus (Acts 26:14)?
options: It is hard to kick against the goads
Your yoke is heavy and your burden unbearable
Too much wine makes a man courageous

question: What did Governor Festus exclaim was driving Paul insane (Acts 26:24)?
What question did Paul ask King Agrippa directly (Acts 26:27)?
options: Do you understand what I am saying?

question: What conclusion did King Agrippa arrive at after interviewing Paul (Acts 26:31)?
options: Paul had not done anything worthy of death or imprisonment
Paul was out of his mind and should be released
Paul had violated the traditions of the Jews which was the cause of the riot

question: Who were Paul and the prisoners headed for Italy handed over to (Acts 27:1)?
options: Lysias, the commander of the tenth legion
Julius, a centurion who belonged to the Imperial Regiment
Claudius Lysias, a centurion who was part of the temple guard

question: As they put out of sea who joined those sailing with Paul (Acts 27:2)?
What kindness did Julius show Paul at Sidon (Acts 27:3)?
options: He allowed him to preach to those on the boat
He accompanied him to the synagogue there
He allowed him to go to his friends

question: Myra is located in what provincial region (Acts 27:5)?

options: An Ephesian ship headed for Italy
A Caesarean ship sailing for Rome
An Alexandrian ship sailing for Italy
A Cyrene ship sailing for Corinth

question: Where did the majority hope to winter on Paul’s voyage to Rome (Acts 27:12)?

options: A hurricane force wind called a northeaster drove them along
A thunderstorm hit called a “ring of fire” and drove them out to sea
A hailstorm hit from the east driving them beyond Phoenix
A tornado swept them away from the coast of Crete out into the sea

question: Where did the ship’s crew fear the sandbars (Acts 27:17)?
After the third day of the storm, what did the sailors on Paul’s boat do (Acts 27:19)?
options: Threw some of the prisoners overboard

question: On the fourteenth night across what sea were they being blown (Acts 27:27)?
What were the sailors pretending when they let down the lifeboats (Acts 27:30)?
options: That they were cutting them loose from the boat
That they were preparing to let anchors down from the bow of the boat
That they were trying to patch a hole in the boat
That they were getting them ready for the prisoners

question: On what island did Paul and the sailors end up shipwrecked on (Acts 28:1)?
What made the islanders on the island of Malta think Paul was a god (Acts 28:6)?
options: He raised a man who had been drowned in the sea
He spoke in tongues to the natives of the island
He healed a man’s broken foot so he could walk
He was bitten by a viper with no ill effects

question: After leaving the island of Malta, where did Paul put in next (Acts 28:12)?
After leaving Rhegium where was the next place Paul’s ship put in at (Acts 28:13)?
The Jews at Rome reported to Paul all of the following EXCEPT (Acts 28:21f)
options: People everywhere are talking against this sect
They had not received any letters from Judea about Paul’s case

They wanted to hear wanted to hear what Paul’s views were
